name: Networking
description: Concepts, not vendors: addressing, protocols, routing and the plumbing of the internet. 30-question seed set, growing to 300.

options: The manufacturer of a network card
A user account on the internet in practice
The physical location of a computer
A device's network interface on a network
questions.explanation: It's a logical address for reaching an interface — not tied to geography, hardware vendor, or a person.

options: Translate human-friendly names into IP addresses in practice
Block access to known malicious websites
Encrypt traffic between browsers and websites
Assign IP addresses automatically to new devices
questions.explanation: The internet's phone book: you type a name, DNS answers with the address packets actually need.

questions.text: The difference between a switch and a router is that a router…
options: Is simply a faster version of a switch
Connects devices within one local network
Connects different networks together
Only works with wireless connections
questions.explanation: Switches forward frames inside a network; routers move packets between networks — including toward the internet.

options: Retransmits anything that gets lost en route
Guarantees packets arrive in the right order
Is faster but offers no delivery guarantees in practice
Establishes a connection before sending data
questions.explanation: No handshake, no retransmission, no ordering — which is exactly why voice, video and games love it.

options: The priority of a packet inside the router
Which physical socket the cable is plugged into
How many devices share the same connection
Which service or application traffic is for
questions.explanation: One IP address, many conversations: ports let the same machine run a web server on 443 and SSH on 22.

questions.text: A LAN and a WAN differ mainly in…
options: How many devices they can address
Whether they use cables or radio
The brand of equipment involved
The geographic scale they cover
questions.explanation: Local (an office, a home) versus wide (cities, countries) — the technologies follow from the distance.

options: Only used by Apple hardware on a network as a rule
The address websites see when you browse
Assigned by the router every time you connect
A hardware identifier burned into the interface
questions.explanation: Layer-2 identity for the local segment. It normally never leaves your network — websites see your IP instead.

options: Whether a host is reachable and how fast
How much bandwidth a connection offers
Which ports are open on a remote host
The full route packets take to a host in practice
questions.explanation: An ICMP echo and its reply: reachability plus round-trip time, nothing more — and some hosts ignore it.

questions.text: Standard ports for HTTP and HTTPS are…
options: 25 and 110 respectively
80 and 443 respectively
21 and 22 respectively
53 and 8080 respectively
questions.explanation: 80 for plain HTTP, 443 for TLS — the pair worth memorising; 21/22 are FTP/SSH, 25/110 mail, 53 DNS.

questions.text: Compared with Wi-Fi, a wired Ethernet connection generally offers…
options: Encryption applied to every single frame
Lower and more consistent latency
Automatic failover between access points
More mobility but much higher latency
questions.explanation: No radio contention, interference or roaming: the wire wins on stability, which matters more than raw speed.

options: The subnet is the 24th in that address block
The first 24 bits identify the network part in practice
Packets may take at most 24 router hops
The network can hold exactly 24 devices
questions.explanation: CIDR notation: 24 network bits leave 8 for hosts — 254 usable addresses in this case.

questions.text: DHCP's job on a network is to…
options: Hand out IP configuration to devices automatically
Resolve names into addresses for clients in practice
Encrypt traffic between clients and gateway
Block rogue devices from joining the LAN
questions.explanation: Discover, offer, request, acknowledge: address, mask, gateway and DNS servers, leased without human effort.

options: Which DNS server is authoritative for this zone?
Which route leads to this remote network?
Which port is this application listening on?
Which MAC address owns this IP on my segment?
questions.explanation: IP gets you to the right network; ARP finds the actual hardware address for the final local hop.

questions.text: NAT allows a home network to…
options: Share one public IP among many private devices in practice
Encrypt all outbound traffic automatically
Assign public addresses to every device inside
Prioritise gaming traffic over downloads
questions.explanation: The router rewrites addresses and tracks connections — one public IP, a whole houseful of devices behind it.

questions.text: The TCP three-way handshake sequence is…
options: HELLO, then KEY, then DATA
REQUEST, then GRANT, then BEGIN
SYN, then SYN-ACK, then ACK in practice
ACK, then SYN, then SYN-ACK
questions.explanation: Synchronise sequence numbers in both directions before any data flows — the price of reliability.

questions.text: In the OSI model, IP addressing and routing live at…
options: Layer 7, the application layer
Layer 4, the transport layer
Layer 3, the network layer
Layer 2, the data link layer
questions.explanation: L2 = frames and MACs, L3 = packets and IPs, L4 = TCP/UDP and ports, L7 = the application itself.

options: Sending packets with increasing TTLs and reading who complains
Asking each router along the path to identify itself
Querying the routing table of the destination host in practice
Flooding all paths and timing which reply comes first
questions.explanation: TTL=1 dies at the first hop, TTL=2 at the second… each expiry message reveals one router on the path.

options: Encrypt traffic between switches automatically
Bond several links into one faster connection
Split one physical switch into isolated segments
Increase the wireless range of access points in practice
questions.explanation: Logical separation on shared hardware: finance and guests on the same switch, unable to see each other.

questions.text: An A record and a CNAME record differ in that a CNAME…
options: Holds arbitrary text such as verification tokens
Lists the mail servers for the domain
Maps a name to an IPv6 address instead of IPv4
Points a name at another name, not an address in practice
questions.explanation: A = name to IP; CNAME = alias to canonical name. (AAAA is IPv6, MX is mail, TXT is the text one.)

options: How many resolvers may cache the record at once
How long resolvers may cache the answer in practice
The maximum hops a query may travel
How often the zone file must be re-signed
questions.explanation: Long TTLs mean fewer queries but slow changes; before a migration you drop the TTL, then flip the record.

options: The mechanism DNS uses to find authoritative servers
The path-vector protocol gluing autonomous systems together
A secure replacement for OSPF in enterprise cores
The protocol that load-balances traffic inside data centres
questions.explanation: Networks (ASes) advertise reachable prefixes and pick paths by policy, not just distance — the internet's routing fabric.

options: A host randomly selects one of several addresses in practice as a rule
One address is announced from many locations; routing picks the nearest
A packet is duplicated to every host in a group in practice as a rule
Traffic alternates between two redundant paths in practice as a rule in practice
questions.explanation: The same prefix from many sites: users reach the topologically closest instance. How 8.8.8.8 and CDNs feel fast everywhere.

questions.text: Path MTU discovery breaks silently when…
options: Firewalls drop the ICMP 'fragmentation needed' messages
Both endpoints support jumbo frames end to end in practice
The sender's interface MTU is set below 1500 bytes
The route changes while a transfer is in progress
questions.explanation: Without those ICMP signals, big packets vanish into a black hole: small transfers work, large ones hang. Classic.

options: Encrypt the sliding window against tampering
Guarantee equal bandwidth to every connection
Stop senders from overwhelming the network path
Reserve bandwidth ahead of large transfers in practice
questions.explanation: Probe, back off on loss, ramp up again: cooperative restraint is what keeps shared links usable at all.

questions.text: QUIC improves on TCP+TLS mainly by…
options: Cutting handshake round trips and avoiding head-of-line blocking
Removing the need for any encryption handshake in practice in practice
Running over raw IP instead of a transport protocol in practice
Compressing every packet with a shared dictionary in practice
questions.explanation: Built on UDP with TLS integrated: faster setup, independent streams, and connection migration across networks.

options: Falls back to the second path only on failure
Duplicates every packet across two paths for safety
Spreads flows across several equally good paths
Requires all links to come from the same vendor
questions.explanation: Hashing per flow keeps packets of one connection on one path (no reordering) while using all the capacity.

questions.text: A recursive DNS resolver differs from an authoritative server because it…
options: Only answers queries arriving over encrypted transports
Chases the full lookup on behalf of clients and caches results
Holds the master copy of the zone's records in practice as a rule
Is operated exclusively by domain registrars in practice
questions.explanation: Root, TLD, authoritative — the resolver walks the chain so your laptop doesn't have to, then caches per TTL.

options: Configure its own address from router advertisements
Request an address lease from a DHCPv6 server as a rule
Negotiate a shorter prefix with the local router
Translate its IPv6 address for IPv4-only peers
questions.explanation: Router advertises the prefix; the host derives its own interface identifier. Addressing without a DHCP server at all.

questions.text: VXLAN is used in data centres to…
options: Encrypt tenant traffic with per-VLAN keys
Compress east-west traffic between racks as a rule
Replace spanning tree inside a single switch
Stretch layer-2 segments across a layer-3 fabric
questions.explanation: Encapsulating frames in UDP gives ~16M virtual networks over a routed underlay — VM mobility without giant broadcast domains.

questions.text: ARP spoofing lets an attacker on the LAN…
options: Exhaust the DHCP pool so no one gets an address
Answer for the gateway's IP and intercept traffic in practice
Force the switch to broadcast all unicast frames
Overwrite the DNS cache of every local client in practice
questions.explanation: Gratuitous ARP replies poison neighbours' tables: traffic for the gateway flows through the attacker first. Dynamic ARP inspection is the switch-side counter.
